text: Terrible WiFi. It’s 2024, there shouldn’t be downtimes in internet service EVERY DAY FOR HOURS. PLEASE DONT CHOOSE THIS PROVIDER!!

I cannot express my disgust with the new t mobile adequately, but will try. I was a customer several years ago, and T Mo had great regional CS who answered within a few every time. For personal reasons ( left the country) I disconnected, then returned when back in the states. MY GOD. the new cs system is the worst WITH any corporation I know of. Since buying a phone (trade-in, but they have lied repeatedly that they never received it) I have had to contact CS 6 times, each time literally an hour wait for a sweet Phillipina, who promises solutions, which rarely appear. Not their fault, I'm not doing some racist stuff here, just TERRIBLE CORPORATE DIRECTION results. I Am now trying to unlock the paid for phone I bought from them 2 months ago, and none of the site stuff works, 2 CS agents ( 2 hours wait) are unable to unlock, now advised to wait 48 hours and call back. What a lesson in enshitification of a previously good company. Stay away!

I had them for about 3 years and my mom lost her phone. I called to see if they were offering any promotions on the phones. They said we I was eligible for free upgrade. We sent 4 phones to get 4 new phones. They can’t find 2 and they put me in as a promotion to by when get one free with the additional line. So instead of paying for the tax I’m now paying for 6 lines of services and 2 new phones. So my bill went from paying 127 a month I am now payin 222 per month. Every time I call 1-3 times per month. The same thing happens every time. A supervisor tells me they are going to escalate it and get back to me and no one ever does. Stay away from them!

Unbelievable ! Wanted to switch from Verizon to t mobile. T mobile wanted to check my credit score !!! Have never seen this!! How can I allow you to check and hurt my credit score to get phone line or just to switch !!? You get a new customer and I have to pay for it by hurting my credit!! Unbelievable. I would never recommend t mobile to anyone
Worst there is! They don't help unless it's taking your money! Be careful they steal your hard earned money!!! 🤬

I switched from Verizon to T-mobile, they offered a $200 gift card for the mobile phone switch and a $50 card for going with their home internet. A year later still don't have either. Provided them the rebate codes THEY provided me, said the $200 card will ship soon, a month later, nothing. The home internet works well, the phone coverage and service isn't as good as Verizon. Customer service is the same.

They are so desperate to take there long time customers and lose them then have a respectable business.  Pay my bill every month through my bank on 13th and last month June 2024 the charged me a late fee $7, but not any other months has it been late. T-Mobile tatic to make you pay on their website or set up where these fraud folks can automatically debit your account. They make mistakes and then how would they rectify correctly in a timely manner, they won't b/c they don't give a crap!!

I can have 5g internet both mobile and in home wifi with perfect connection and somehow unable to get anything done cause its always loading and even received calls and missed them the voicemail comes thru a day late i cannot express enough that tmobile is just garbage

Business account terror line of credit stolen after going online to purchase devices for my new business account was asked to provide ID and business info and was approved for $5000 for business credit and when I was asked to pay the sales tax swiped my card for $358 the store Rep had to call customer service and verify my bank account over the phone with BOA business. Once completed the rep on the phone stated he would complete the transaction and send a digital receipt to my email. He told the store rep to wait a few mins. for it to go active when she logged back in it showed I only had a $1500 business credit and he closed the first account and created a new one now the devices I was already approved for I can no longer get because my credit limit has decreased and after 3 weeks of talking to any and everyone no one seems to be able to get my business credit back on top of that 20 days later I received a bill for 6 phone lines with no devices and 3 different account open in my business name so a total of 9 lines no devices but I’m being billed customer service sucks and they will hang up when you try to get to the bottom of things.

I recently visited T-Mobile in Ridgeland on County Line Road. Customer service was amazing Sanyonette was awesome. She helped me every step of the way getting my new iPhone 15 set up she even applied the screen protector. My billing was completely explained and I encourage everyone to visit this location. And please ask for Sanyonette.

I recently wrote a review of Assurant, the company that does T-Mobile's insurance company. It wasn't a good one. I didn't really have any problem with T-Mobile, I had been with them for 2 yrs. After getting bent over by Assurant I reached out to T-Mobile thinking maybe they could help me. Their response was to inform me that I got bent over deal with it. Well I decided I would just buy another galaxy out of my own pocket but it just rubbed me wrong. So fighter pilots I'm now a straight talk wireless customer. T-Mobile has no appreciation for a good customer and they are as sorry as Assurant. Like peas in the same rotten pod
Tmobile has gone down hill. They rip you off, service isn't good, add on fees , etc. All the negative reviews are true! This company was good back in 2014, but Now No!!!

edits.text: Horrible company. Tmobile use to be good, but the company is declining.  Horrendous customer service, ripping people off, shutting down a customer's phone due to your incompetence. Keep posting consumers.

text: I have been with T-Mobile for 15 years. Originally, I was delighted with them. I recommended all of the people I knew to them. However, well before covid, their prices increased and their service decreased. Where before, an issue could be resolved with a call to customer service within minutes, the last dozen calls I have made have required multiple calls that take anywhere from 30 - 90 minutes to get a human being. Most recently, I received a $300 charge for a piece of supplemental equipment I had used since they did not have coverage in the location of my rural home and workplace, which I returned when I moved to the city. That return was not documented, and now, six years later, I am being informed of the misfile  technology that I returned.

I am suicidal because of this company.  This company has taken everything from me.. my job. I'm about to be homeless in a month. I can't return a 800$ bike. I've lost 20 pounds because I can only afford to eat once a day. The reason is because I dropped my phone in the water. Had insurance got a new phone. They don't have a store around here (wish I would have been informed) in fact it's a different state. They can't send an activation code to my phone because the sim isn't activated makes no sense. I can't go out of state to get a frighten code because I am disabled. They are going back and forth back and forth. Telling me they guarantee it's fixed to telling me there's no way around it. It's making things that much worse with my mental health when they do this to me. The reason I cannot access all my accounts is because I changed every account to just one password. I forgot to write them down and was going to  but then i dropped my phone in water. worked as a 3rd party Amazon seller (job lost) apartment (I'll be homeless in a month) rapid weightloss (lost 20 pounds in the past month because I can no longer afford to eat) I cannot access my home security system (which is awesome because there's been an influx of break ins where I live) I don't know what I'm gonna do so I can only see one way out. This company has taken everything from me. I've never been suicidal in my life and now I have it all planned out. I'm making arrangements to give all my stuff away and just let my family know just how much I love them. I never thought getting a phone would be the reason I leave this world. I've never experienced worse customer service in my life. They really could care less about the customer and only care about money.

The reception and reliability was great- otherwise I would only have rated T-Mobile 1 star. I signed up for a more expensive cell service plan (2 lines 55+ for $110) in order to obtain a discount on internet service. There was a promo for a certificate for a free Amazon TV when signing up (you will have to google free Amazon TV Christmas 2023 since I can't put a link in this review)  I signed up before the deadline, filled out everything required, paid my bill timely, then waited and waited, still no TV. I checked with customer service and was informed that I should have the certificate in 60-90 days. After a while, I noticed a lot of complaints from disappointed customers who also did not receive the promised TV. I was happy with my service so I gave up chasing the freebie. About 4 months in, I received an email telling me that I had to verify that we qualified for the old fart program- I thought I did that when I placed my order so I chatted with customer service who informed me that I had to go to the store for verification. I wasted a lunch hour driving to the store, only to be told that I would have to contact customer service because that was not something that could be done in store. This month's bill was over $50 more than last month's, I am assuming the senior discount was removed. I just cancelled my service and switched to a better rated provider, my cost is about the same and their service reviews are much, much higher than T-mobile.

Tmobile is a fraud. There a serial rip off to the public and they need to be shut down for taking advantage of customers and price gouging there contracts and basically popular for ripping people off and up selling there products in ways that leave consumers in debt. They also are extremely disrespectful when your bill is due and they literally will lie on the automated line saying there customer service is closed when my prepaid bill lapsed but any other time they were open when my bill was active lmfao. This is a seriously evil and screwed up cut throat company and i can't wait until tmobile dies.

They lied about the plan they promised and then had the gall to steal my trade in. Every time you call in its the same 1hr conversation explaining the problem all over as if no one has written a single note of previous conversations. They promised a new S24 and payoff my trade in. Got the new S24 and they are not honoring the trade as they said time ran out! But its THEM screwing the order up twice not me!! I said send me my phone back then..reply...NO! I have never been so livid nor had such horrible service from a company. Most untrained unprofessional staff EVER!! I am going elswhere as soon as my new $400 S24 is unlocked. What a joke!

They tried to change me 3x the restocking fee snd force me to spend more money in an upgrade
They lied about the features on an Apple Watch which brought about the return 
I had to call customer service to get anything done
The assistant mgr Marcus leads the way with lies and misinformation

My wife had a Galaxy Fold 4 with a malfunctioning screen. The phone has never been damaged, but despite this, they refused a warranty exchange saying she was responsible. The screen going wonky is a known problem but they law it on the consumer saying they damaged it. Stay away from T-Mobile . . . don't walk away, RUN. I will never do business with T-Mobile ever again.

All I need is access to my online account.  THEY CANT DO IT.  They have had multiple people make really stupid attempts at fixing the issue but.....THEY CANT DO IT and this is a commercial account.
Pathetic

T Mobile will extract as much money as possible from you. The company is not trusted or truthful in any sense. They promised me a great deal on a phone if I sent mine in any condition. After they received it they said the screen was cracked and will charge me an additional $26 a month for 24 months. They lied!

I had T-Mobile 5G Home Internet for several months before the defective router kept disconnecting and rebooting. I canceled the service and returned the router. Customer service never mentioned anything more was due. 14 days later I was charged the usual month internet fee of $50 and customer service said I had to pay for the complete billing cycle. They wouldn't even give a credit for the unused internet. I considered this a form of theft!
Stole $1000 dollars from my account on auto pay. They admit they made a mistake but keep telling me to call Ass-u-ran Insurance. Tmobile took the money from my account.

In April 2022, while in Georgia for work, I went to a T-Mobile store to inquire about a wifi hotspot. The representative there told me about a small wifi device they had. I asked if I would be able to try out the device and T-Mobile’s service to see if it would actually work where I was and if it didn’t work, return it. He said yes. 

I signed up and took the device home. After trying the device and service out for 5 days, I was not able to access the internet using it. So, I returned to the store where the same representative helped me. After returning the device, he told me I would not be charged anything else and my service was canceled. 

For a year following that day, T-Mobile charged me $50 every month. I called them several times to request that they stop withdrawing from my account and refund the money they had taken. They told me that I had to go into a store to be helped with this. 

So, I went into a store. At the store, they tried to help me by calling T-Mobile’s Care department. At that point, they said that T-Mobile Care would be taking care of it - that I would not be charged anymore and I would receive a refund. That did not happen. 

I followed up with the store manager after yet another payment was taken out of my account. By this time it was March 2023, almost 1 year after the first charge! I never received a response from her. 

So, in late March of 2023, I went to my bank and asked that they place a stop payment on T-Mobile’s monthly draft from my account. They did that and refunded a portion of the money that T-Mobile wrongfully took from my account. 

T-Mobile has now (as of 6/17/24) reported me to a collection agency stating that I owe them $378! I do not owe T-mobile any money and in fact, they still owe me over $300! 

I wish I would have thought to place the stop payment with my bank after the first fraudulent charge by T-Mobile. Regardless, beware of T-Mobile.

Worst customer service. I have called about the same technical issue multiple times, at least twice a month for the last 6 months, and spent at least 3 hours every time I called ... No technical knowledge what so ever, no professionalism, and a total waste of time and money ...
There ad is correct! Ordered Home Internet, received at front door in 2 days and had set up under 15 minutes. Was able to connect my two TV’s, echo dot,  tablet and phone. So far so good!

Have been a customer with t-mobile since they started. They were recently bought over. Now my reception  has a lot of areas that don't come in where it did before. My data was unlimited to a point and would then slow down,in the 15 years I've been with boost this only occurred a couple of times. Now it's every month. And with the new owners it doesn't slow down,it stops!!!!! NO DATA AT ALL. This is a breach in my original contract stating a slow down, not a complete stop, meaning you can't get any data use on the road.You were always able to see how much data you had left before the slow down would acura. Now you can't check what you have left and it just stops working. This is the perfect situation for a class action law suit. Anybody on board,count me
In, it's not about the money,it's about being a loyal customer and a new company taking over and now trying to get more money out of you to increase their profits. Verizon may charge more, but the service is the best!!!

If it was possible to give this company NO STARS that’s what they would earn. No stars. They lie to their customers. They use deceptive and discriminatory practices in everyday business. If I could switch carriers I would. It’s not because of the service. It’s because of the customer service, management, and C Level Executives that continue to let this crap go on. They only care about how much money you can give them. They don’t care about their customers. Hence their 1.3 stars rating.  Their trash. Simply put.

T-Mobile ripped me off. The best way to get a real response from T-Mobile for a complaint for getting ripped off is to email the CEO then  the most important thing is put in a complaint to the FCC. T-Mobile executive team must reach out to you try to resolve and then respond to the FCC within thirty days. The links for both is:  put a .com at the end of his email Mike.Sievert@T-Mobile   He is the CEO. Read this about what you file a complaint about. Then go to the FCC site and file a complaint.  Hope this helps.

T-Mobile should not be trusted. While I was excited to transfer from Verizon to T-Mobile because of all the benefits and credits that I would receive, it seems that they lie and will bait you to switch and state you do not qualify for the promotions they explicitly state you will receive. I have never had a phone carrier to lie just to get you to switch from another carrier. Should have just stayed with Verizon or considered AT&T. I was told that I would receive a port-in credit which at the time that I was told, the promotion was already expired but this is what the representative sold me on in order to switch carriers, in addition the T-Mobile Representative sent me the required forms to submit to receive the credit, even offered tips on how to submit them successfully. While the promotion was being processed I called multiple times for status updates and the representative assured me that I would receive the credit, just give it time to process. Later, that promotion was denied due to it being out of the promotion period. Yes, this is an error on that T-Mobile representative's part, which the T-Mobile Care Team acknowledged, however, they do nothing to honor this.

Had T-Mobile Internet. Was unable to get unlimited even though on waiting list 4 times. When notified by app notification that it was tried to sign up but was declined. Found service with no data cap and returned router. First tried to charge me for damage but router was never moved in my possession. Then charged me for normal wear when they specifically say there is no extra charge for equipment. With data caps and them going back on their promises I cannot recommend their service at all. Stay away if you have any other options

Long story-read if you don't want this to happen to you. When issues go on endlessly for years – it's not just a once or twice fluke nor can tower location always be to blame. So tired of hearing, “it's the towers” “it's the tower location”. Then fix tower problem or stop selling in this area.
      I know some people say it works so good. Some win the lottery too. Doesn't mean it's a common occurrence! Looking at tmobile rating here seems the chance of it being “so good” is same as hitting lottery jackpot. Millions to one! I truly don't know where tmobile gets those best of stats. NO ONE I mention tmobile to has anything good to say about them.
     Unfortunately we got it all – internet, 2 phones & watches. Tmobile internet & cell service is unreliable. No matter if home, out locally or some other city we could not count on having reliable cell service. We had 2 identical phones & side by side could have different connectivity issues. 1 working, other might not. Might work one trip, not the next. Not always same phone or in same location – just whenever either couldn't connect. Phones also would say no internet connection available when it should be cell not WiFi anyway. Later in same location it would work. Cant find directions or businesses without connection. At times no cell service at all, was garbled or dropped calls. 
    With so many problems & internet outages I took photos & videos. Basically anytime you turned on a device requiring tmobile you knew it would probably lose connection at least once or something else. An hour show might go out 5 or 6 times, every day & at all times of the day. My photo file got so large I had to stop. Local office still looked at me as if I had 2 heads even after viewing videos of what I was telling them. Didn't care.
    Being so unreliable, I would not give a child a tmobile phone. I'd also discourage travelers especially those who go alone or need to rely on cell service. 
      For travel safety & being locked into a contract, we got a prepaid account from competitor to compare with tmobile. Competitor passed consistently with flying colors, every time & everywhere! Like we knew, it was just tmobile failure. And it's not like we're in some remote part of the world or well below sea level etc. We live in town, not country. We couldn't wait to dump tmobile! We also tested internet – competitor works. They offer free internet booster if it doesn't work throughout house! Price is only a bit more, but we now have an internet system that works. Watching an hour program doesn't take 2 hours now.  Cheaper isn't better if it doesn't reliably work. 
     When phones paid off & the "no contracts" contract part of bill paid off - we canceled all services May 11, 2024. Was paid up to date at that time as of recent auto-pay. Only charges from then to May 11 were now due. Said final bill will be sent. DON'T cancel before you stop auto-pay & remove payment method. It's how they overbill you. Make copies of anything important on your online account. You cannot access your online account once it's canceled. You'll be told changes have to be made online, then told if number canceled, there's no online account now. No one will help. This allows them to create charges & bill since most people don't double check for accuracy.
     Not being able to remove payment method, through BBB contact from tmobile Sr. Specialist Team, CEO. She took auto-pay off & most importantly reviewed account to give what was actually owed. Said made notes on account but was confident final bill would now be correct amount. Told us wait & contact her if amount was different. 
     Arrived 6/5/24 amt almost doubled. Contacted. She got back to us with true amount owed & how to pay now there is no online account access on about 6/6. We paid this amount due within a few days. After all today is only 6/12/24. Wasn't paid today or yesterday.
     Tmobile is horrible!! Recap - Account current. Last auto pay 4/21 next would've been 5/21 – but canceled tmobile 5/11. Received final bill abt 6/5 still wrong amount due – contacted home office immediately & paid within days of actual amount due. Today – 6/12, just a month from our cancellation date - I receive a letter from collection agency dated 6/5 on behalf of tmobile for a crazy amount & fee on top of it for service on a paid up to date account that WE cancelled. Date of their letter shows tmobile had to contact them well before 6/5 for agency to create/mail me letter dated 6/5! Really? I am furious!!
     I work hard on my credit rating (another stupid thing for only businesses to legally way overcharge consumer since it's damned if you pay your bills off & damned if you don't) to have a company like this so blatantly abuse the system!!
    For mobile/internet service that is reliable & works - forget tmobile exists! - Just remember t-mobile service (for me) stands for Terrible Mobile Service! Go elsewher

This place is terrible I made a settlement to pay my T Mobile home Internet now when I call they hang the phone on my phace I make settlement and I pay now they say I have to pay more 130,00 ?? I am taking you all to court I am so tired of this company bulshit

T-Mobile customer service is a total fiasco! I moved away from T-Mobile when the company I work for introduced us to Previ. Previ still uses T-Mobile service but at a much discounted rate for corporations. During the transition I overpaid my final bill which was reflected in the last two statements. I tried the online chat feature of which they asked for my PIN to access my account. Of course they  said I had the wrong PIN# which is the PIN I had been using for years, and years as a T- mobile customer. I was told I would need to call the customer service line to get this resolved. After 30 minutes on hold I spoke to the rep and they told me I was out of luck if I didn't have the PIN. I was told I would need to drive to a T-mobile store and reset the PIN with picture ID, which I did. I then returned home and called customer service back with my new PIN in hopes of finally getting my overpayment back. Well it wasn't that easy, I was told to give the PIN and she said it was the wrong PIN.... LOL you cant make this up! She then told me I needed to call a different number which I repeated back to her because I had never heard of a number starting with 999! She assured me it was right but to call it tomorrow due to the long hold times. Well my intuition was right and she sent me to a non-existent number. I once again called the customer service line at which I spent another 20-30 minutes on hold, explained what had happened and my frustration with it taking me three days to get this resolved and still no help! So in total I was about 4 hours and having to drive to a store to reset a PIN that they said was wrong to get a 95.00 credit! This not the first time that I have experienced this with Tmobile. Shame on me for that but there will not be a third time! If the credit is not in my account in ten days I will camp on the store doorstep and have a conversation with every customer that is considering t-mobile!
T-mobile billing department has been annoying, dishonest, incompetent and harassing us!

The Worst !!! I don't have coverage at my house I don't have coverage at work !!! I pay $93 a month for one line I paid extra for insurance and when my phone broke and I filed a claim to get a new one they sent me some off brand that's all messed up and customer service is a joke and no help for anything .
I have been extremely happy with service and pricing.  Everything I was promised was delivered. Easy to reach customer service and every time my issue was resolved to my satisfaction

Was told I would have great service in my area. Called the next day of getting the service bc we had no service, they told me a tower was down. We r in month 3 and guess what they still say tower is messed up. Can’t wait for my 2 years is up. I am out and will never use this service again.

The way T mobile handled my situation they do t even deserve the 1star!  When I initially ordered the phone I was happy with the amount I was going to spend on the iPhone 13 and the plan I chose.  When I got the email that it was shipped I checked the tracking immediately to see how long I would be without a phone.  When I saw the comments from ups it stated that they recieved and empty package and three away the box and was reporting the shipment damaged to t mobile.  I immediately called.  I was at work it was around 630 pm at 1am I was still on the phone trying to get another one.  They canceled my order and started a entire new contract.  It was never the same as the 1st.  I waited for the email to electronically sign I never got it then had to call again the next day.  When I called they said the second contract had been canceled due to no electronical signature how can I sign something I never got! So then a third cat contract with generated and it was never the same of course as a first one why I don't know but they were supposed to fix it did they no! So then I was in Hawaii when I got my bill and it was $400 and something dollars and I was on vacation I called they told me I had to send the phone back and that they had to send me another one why couldn't they just fix it with all the things that they can do nowadays and it's 2024 they just couldn'tType a few things on their computer and do it right or make it right no I had to send it from Hawaii back to T-Mobile and they sent me another one so here's my fourth contract still wasn't the same as the first one I had to call every single month after that to get my bill adjusted to the correct amount I was first promised every month finally on the 3rd or 4th month I said I am done I am done I'm tired of calling you all can't make this right I don't want to be a part of T-Mobile anymore so they took it back even though it had been more than the 30 days or whatever and that made me happy I was glad I was unhappy I had to send it back and start another phone deal but I was happy that they were taking this one back and I was getting away from T-Mobile and now they're saying I owe them over $100 induce for my bill but they kept my $116 that was supposed to be a refund and I have the emails and everything to prove it all I have conversations after conversations that they could all listen to but do they ever no they don't if are you I would stay far far away from T-Mobile because they're very unprofessional and they break their promises!   I can not believe they are one of the top sellers with the experience I had and am STILL having with T mobile!!! Horrible service

Called about a automated message that keeps calling me 20 times or more a day for over a month.  The first person I talked to recommended their scam shield, I told them I have it and use it but all that does is send it to my voicemail and that still alerts me I have voice mail all hours of the day and night.  I asked to speak with level 2 support, they put me in hold for about 30 minutes when finally I got a ringing sound and then a message stating they can't take my call.  Excellent customer support (not), they couldn't even come back on the line to say they would call me back or anything.  After being a customer for over 20 years I'll be moving to Verizon this weekend.  T-Mobile doesn't care about their customers at all.

I went to get an account with T-Mobile. The guys in the store told me I needed to call customer care. I went to my car to make this call. The guys said it would be quick. The robot at the beginning of the call said 30 minute wait time. It was an entire hour! And then the customer care guy said he needed to talk to the guys in the store. The call dropped just as I walked into the store. They guys in the store just shrugged. I walked out in frustration. A few weeks later, I got a bill for my supposed service. It was only for $5.65, but I feel it just adds insult to injury.

I recently transferred 4 lines from Verizon to T-Mobile. I have to say, my experience with T-Mobile so far has been fantastic! I've had several reasons to contact T-Mobile customer service during the process (billing, phone reimbursement, etc). Outside of an hour plus waiting for their tranfer department, contacting customer service has been very responsive and pleasant. The whole process has been seamless. Several years ago I went through the same process transferring from Visible to Verizon. Verizon was an absolute nightmare! I literally spent 30+ hours chatting and on hold trying to resolve the myriad of issues they couldn't seem to get corrected. Kudos to T-Mobile for their excellent customer service in an age when everyone else is sacrificing quality service for a buck.

customer service is the worst.  I have been a sprint customer since 1998 then they went to tmobile.Absolutely the worst experience ever.  They said they have no account associated with my phone number, well you take my automatic payment for 25 years.  This was my last draw with them.  And if you call customer service expect a 30 minute wait.  Then they direct you to a store.  Now the acquired another company this is just great, and now try to get someone who speaks the same english you speak.

First of all I’m not happy I had to give any stars at all. T-Mobile has become the worse mobile carrier. I purchased a phone a year ago paid cash and 30 days later phone was not working properly so I call T-Mobile. They sent me a refurbished phone after I just gave them almost 1300 dollars for a new phone. Over the last 8 months I’ve had 4 refurbished phones because they don’t work properly. I honor my part of our contract which is paying but T-Mobile doesn’t provide me with a working phone nor service. I’ve had only 2 bars on this phone since I’ve received it and now I’m on hold to get refurbished phone sent out to me again. 5 phones in one year. I’m now looking into switching with another company.

I received an e-mail that informed me my monthly bill will go up by $2. That was last week. Today I see news of T-Mobile is acquiring U.S. Cellular for $4.4b. That explains the $2/mo increase… I, and others, are paying for T-Mobile’s acquisition costs. Outrageous! Insulting. I’m infuriated by this garbage. And should I find a better plan, you bet I’m going to switch. If I’m paying more for the acquisition of another cellular provider, I should receive stock + dividends. I’m just a pawn ♟️ in this larger chess game. Wish I had attorneys who’d look out for me, and Sue T-Mobile ~ formerly Primeco. I’d like to kick that pink alien 🤖 phone in the circuits.

If I could give them a 0. I would they said that they were cheaper than Att and  what I got a phone And 2 watches, I open them. It was a hard time switching my phone number over from ATt And I did this in January of 2024 I wrapped the stuff up. Sent it right back that same day. They receive the items. They said that I owed them $350. In actuality, they owed me $240 for my taxes. They sent me to collections, and now I'm having to dispute this before they put it on my credit. I've talked to T-Mobile numerous times from January. Tell June. Them telling me oh yeah, we're going to send you your money back. You don't owe us anything. We're gonna put a dispute in b******* now. I'm talking to the collection agency because t mobile sent it, to collections. This has been really stressful. I've talked to multiple people at T-Mobile. Even gave them my bank account information. They said that they would put it in my account in 1 to2  days never did. Do not work with T-Mobile. They'll rip you off. And then try to send you 2 collections. I worked really hard on my credit. I have a high credit score, but they don't care

I've been with T-Mobile for 23 years. My phone broke. I went in for another one. They offered me a promotion. My bill went from $85 to a 140 for a promotion. What kind of a promotion is that that you're you're making me think I have a deal but really? I'm gonna pay for it in the long run, so keep your promotions to yourself T-Mobile. Been with you 23 years. I'm about ready to exit

Once again - horrible customer service.  Called to get a device removed from my account.  You get their little recording "let me get you right over to someone who can help" - to then be told that you have about a 2-hour wait or you can elect to get a callback in about "2 hours".  That is ludicrous.  Are you telling me they're so swamped with support calls that they're backed up for 2 hours or do they have a cottage customer service organization of folks in homes now that are taking calls.  And they'll tell you they can't do anything about your bill if you walk into their store.  They're happy to sell you whatever they possibly can, though.  This company has become such a disappointment and frustration from what it was years back.  I wish Verizon home internet would come into our neighborhood because I would drop T-Mobile in a heartbeat - not that they even seem to care anymore.

edits.text: Up until the past couple months we were always extremely pleased with T-Mobile, especially the Internet service.  Over the past couple months since we moved to SW Florida we have had several notifications about tower maintenance to "ensure a great network experience".  I beg to differ.  Last week there was a tower maintenance issue that completely disrupted and slowed our Internet service from 450 down to 12.  While their Internet customer service reps were very friendly, they tried to tell us it was our 5G device while all the while it was due to their tower issues.  We just got another notice about tower maintenance today.  All that seems to do is interrupt and slow down our service for days until it stabilizes. This is not acceptable when one works from home.

text: CUSTOMER CARE WAIT TIME HORRENDOUS AND INCOMPETENT

I would give this a minus star if I  could I had an emergency problem and this phone keeps hanging up its a disaster I recommend any one to research this company before doing business with them it's the worst service ever
Product fine - promotions a joke!  I really thought this was a real and viable firm but very disappointed.
Wow 90 days.

WORST EXPERIENCE! 
I am a brand new member at the excelsior springs, Mo location on Jesse James Rd and within the first 14 days I spoke to many chat reps and made 2 visits to the store. I have kept all the screenshots for evidence. Apparently it’s okay for T-Mobile sales reps to attempt to rip off customers for $750+ plus dollars when I was later told should’ve only been $70 when trying to do a buyer’s remorse. Apparently it’s okay for sales representatives to add a phone line to my account illegally without my permission and access all my banking information and cause my credit to go down 27 points. I tried to resolve this and T-Mobile ultimately told me they are not doing anything to make it right and that’s it’s my problem. I would not recommend them to anyone.
I've been getting slow/no data service in my area in these past 3 weeks since they didn't say they were fixing/upgrading their cell towers. This stuff is infuriating man.

As a result of many problems dealing with T-Mobile customer service, I successfully got away from T-Mobile a couple of months ago; their billing practices are so miserable, my final invoice showed they owed me $89 and change. Since it's been a couple of months since I got that statement, I called this morning to ask where my money is. I had to spend 17 minutes on the phone to find out the representative had no idea how to issue my refund.
So it's your job to wait when when T-Mobile's customer service doesn't know how to do their jobs. They have to go ask someone, you get to wait and the results you get back may or may not reflect the truth. Yet another reason I'm convinced I did the right thing in leaving T-Mobile.

Katia helped me today to choose a new phone plan and to port my current number over to the new plan.  It took a lot of her time but she still was patient, very courteous and helpful. She methodically went through all my questions explaining everything carefully.  I highly recommend the woodland shop should you need to get a new service. Not only were the other workers helpful as well but Eric even suggested a better deal than  I initially had in mind when I entered the store  Don’t hesitate to use their service. You won’t be disappointed.

The UNCARING Carrier. Support is awful, and slow to respond. I used to have a lifetime price guarantee that they just simply reneged on some time ago (for everyone that had that guarantee too). Now I see a 5 year price guarantee advertised! Do they think that I will believe them ever again with any  guarantee?  For that matter, anything they say at all? Do they think that a 5 year lie sounds more plausible? Does T-mobile think customers are stupid and have no memory? I hate the uncaring carrier and their fake guarantees.

edits.text:
Home Internet: I have had three replacement access points to this date (6 months). My speed initially was 120Mbps. That was fantastic but lasted only the first few weeks. I now receive 20Mbps or less and the access point constantly disconnects from the service/internet and needs rebooted. I have a repeater and even without it receive a good signal. With the repeater I receive Excellent signal. I live two miles from my tower, and can see it from my back yard. The repeater is 30 feet and has an unobstructed view of the tower.  Signal is clearly not my problem. T-mobile has confirmed this via support.

Since my first month, T-mobile has stated that my tower in being upgraded and my poor service is due to that factor.  Six months, limitless calls to support and the "upgrade" has STILL not been completed. I am now told it can take another year!

This is a support, and service nightmare. Broken service and useless support that cannot resolve the issue in a timely fashion. This is not the largest problem or concern however!

I have several friends and family that have t-mobile internet. All of their connections (not the same tower) work as mine used to. It is fine for them, and other than a few issues not too much trouble. I had a friend ask about my service and I told them how bad my  service was but I had friends and family that were fine with theirs. I suggested they give it a try since my tower seems to be the issue.

I take my access point to their location and I receive 120Mbps and an excellent signal. Great, you are in the service area, signal and speed is great, you should get it. 

She cannot. The address is unavailable for service despite it working as I tested it. It is two blocks from a friend who has t-mobile home internet right now!

Must be a mistake. We try several dozens of addresses to she what is up on the website. None of the addresses work. Even my address as well as people I know who have service across two cities.. their addresses don't work either. We have T-mobile internet TODAY, but we could not purchase service today. We try the local T-Mobile store. They stated  that if the address does not work, they cannot sell the router. I explain the issue we were having, and they don't look interested. When asked when they last sold a router , management suddenly intervened and refused to answer the question. Suspicious!

I then went online and found service areas t-mobile had advertised in West Virginia available since 2020. I then collaborated with newspapers stories about service available in multiple cities via google to PROVE the addresses in these cities should work. I used a tower locator website, and zillow to find addresses with 5G coverage and home internet advertised within two miles of towers. EVERY address attempted (hundreds of them) in Huntington, Wheeling, Charleston, Parkersburg, Vienna, Fairmont, etc. failed but should have been available. Suspicious!

Support calls, and store again had no explanation and was completely ignorant of this fact. 

I ask you: does this sound like an upgrade? Degraded service for half a year and no "fix" in sight? No ETA for the upgrade?  ALL service is now unavailable for purchase over the entire state, and not a soul has any explanation? 

I try addresses regularly, and all of them fail and have for weeks now. It is not a mistake on the website in my opinion. I am now going to try cities in other states in proximity (Ohio and Kentucky for example) to our supposed service areas. I don't have an answer as to what is going on with my service or what is going on with T-Mobile home internet services. I suspect something is exceptionally wrong. That is fine, whatever. But I feel lied to, cheated, and I really don't like no answers given to me from people who SHOULD have all of the answers.

Support from "experts" is the worst experience of any company I have ever had in my life consistently. Telephone support: on hold as people are calling and keeping  the lines/support personnel busy asking about their late iphone deliveries. Gone on for months now! Use a different number for this! Legitimate support waiting in line for Karen crying about their late iphone is not support. Once connected the "experts" will always transfer you repeatedly to get to an "expert" that can solve your problem! What geniuses. see what I did there? Yea. Don't have the audacity to name support people experts or geniuses when they clearly have IQ less than 70. Chat support: Same issue. Can't help you. Ever. I have attempted to use this with less than stellar results too many times.  Chat should not take hours between responses! Yet it consistently does. No resolution and must make a call in all cases for me. Why even bother if that is always the result?

Signal/Marketing. Hype does not match their claims of signal or speed. I travel and find that no matter where I go... I am not at all happy with anything from t-mobile. Support, speed or service. 

Technical: Dropped calls, unable to make calls ( to be fair I came from AT7T.. it WAS much better where I live and go. Going back to them.

SMS: fails 50% of the time sending while on wifi. MUST turn off wifi calling/wifi connection to send texts with all 6 of my phones! My entire family wants me dead because I switched to T-Mobile. I am not even kidding. I am a pariah as a result. Frankly, I am sick of the complaints every day from everyone. Think I will get rid of T-Mobile and the family too. 

ALL support requests (including fixing a few phones that  call and text from the wrong number- and yet receive the calls and texts sent to them) have gone on deaf ears. Two months and still not a single solution to any of my myriad of problems. As I said... AT&T... I had no idea how good I had it.

text: They seemingly lie about stuff and hold the phone hostage when you cancel their service lock the phone even though it's paid off.

I've spent more than 2 months trying to resolve an over-billing issue - including speaking to at least 2 supervisors, who promised to review the account and call me back in 24 to 48 hours. Never received a call back and the issue is still open. Incredibly bad customer service - time to find a new carrier!

Having a worst experience with T mobile, the horrible customer service ever. We ordered 3 lines and new phones which were not delivered to us as no one was at home, they received the devices back. Still we being charged for more than $500 initial fees and calling them for refund from past one month. Adding to that we received mobile service invoice for $278 today for the service we never used. Really!!! It’s a nightmare. Please be cautious while switching to T-mobile.

T-mobile is THE WORST mobile service company around. I quit them in April to go back to Spectrum and they were still charging me for Ipad services, would not consider a one and last time courtesy leniency. The customer service is substandard, and no matter what promotion they are offering, you'll wind up being taxed even after you complete your plan commitment.

T moble agebt in May 2023 informed me that my apple tv was up and i would have to pay to keep. Now i been paying and tmoble is now saying no it on us for lifetime. Agent i was talking to did not document anyyhing we talked about. also new agent today just aburtly transfered me to tech support. Tech support was in dark why i was transfered. I was in dark i was being transfered. Such bad customer service.
The worst company and customer service!  ! Just for transfer phone number to another customer it took a forever ! Waiting 6 hours on the phone! Never recommend this company! Be careful guys!

They can start by being truthful about coverage area before a customer signs up. They told me my area is covered by their internet service and phone service but the internet is horribly slow (about 3 mbps - they advertised 40 mbps) and the cell coverage is very spotty and both are heavily effected by the weather. I consider the sales folks to be deceitful in their advertising practices. In my case they did  fulfill their service and coverage promises.

T-Mobile sales dept is deceitful. I transferred from Verizon thinking I was saving $50 only to find out that my free watches are not free. I am paying for the watches and I am now paying for two additional lines. These two additional lines were never discussed during my phone conversation. T-Mobile refuses to do anything to rectify this situation. I clearly stated my desire to have a lower bill and was deceived.

My complaint for Tmobile is that I don't think their sales associates are as concerned with helping customers as they are with making sales. I was suckered into a "free" smart watch a few years ago. I had to pay $40 to have it activated and then a $15 a month payment was added to my monthly bill. I could not have this removed for 2 years unless I was willing to pay the full price of the watch. They would not let me return it even after only one week.
  My second and more recent complaint is with my experience yesterday. I went in to upgrade my phone. Initially I was told that I would only have to pay sales tax, but because my screen was cracked my phone had no value as a trade-in. Which is fine because I've been paying their $18 a month insurance since 2017. I mentioned this to the sales associate, and instead of helping me use the insurance, which she is equipped to do, she instead tried to pitch selling me the new phone for full price with a more expensive plan. This would be ignorant to do, and not in my best interest, as I can just wait a few days for the insurance to send me a replacement to use as a trade-in.
      These big cellphone companies are starting to get a little too predatory for my liking, and I've decided to start looking into alternatives such as PureTalk.

T-Mobile blows chunks..,!!
Unfortunately this review panel makes you at least give a start even be able to put in a review but as far as I'm concerned T-Mobile is the worst and will always be the worst they ruined my business.......
Their salespeople are snakes....
 they're liars cheats and thieves just like all of them.......
.I wasn't able to access any of my emails phone calls because of their two step verification system on my iPhone and then took me to court to prove all this with their legion of lawyers instead of helping their customers. I'm sure I'm not the first and I definitely won't be the last as far as I can tell this company's motto should be we don't give one rat's ass about any of you. Give us your money cuz that's all we care about.

T-Mobile Business Internet is (still) a mess and a joke.

As both a T-Mobile Home Internet user AND T-Mobile Business Internet user, it is astounding how different the user experience is for both products.  Through my experience, it is straight forward and simple on the T-Mobile Home Internet side to have questions answered, access my account, and to ultimately receive internet.

On the contrary, the business process is a mess.  For one, you work through a representative, which sounds good in theory to have that personal experience; however, that representative changed hands 4 times in the first year.  So, when they "accidentally" sent me 3 boxes and charged me separately for all 3 lines, you would think they'd have documentation and process in place for handing things off when a representative changes.  Nope.  Not at all.  Not only did it require multiple e-mails and phone calls to explain the situation, the previous representative was told to "no longer work my account" by their manager, so they couldn't step in and help if they wanted to (so I'm told).  Just an odd way to conduct business if you want to provide that "personal" touch through representatives.

Adding another layer of poor a poor user experience.  There is a business support line (which, makes sense as representatives don't want to handle the simple day-to-day things like changing a password), but the issue here is the inconsistency of both sides forwarding you to the other for things that are above a password reset and below needing a new product.  It's incredibly frustrating as a user of this product.

Oh - one more example of how poor the business home internet processes are.  When selling my business and changing hands, instead of transferring the my device and my account to the new owner, they sent him a new device and added that device onto my account.  So although I was able to cancel my device, my account remained active receiving charges from the new device they sent the new business owner.  All through this, no communication was received on my end.

It's just shocking to me how poor the Business Internet process and experience is relative to the Home Internet.  Could not contrast more.
They are not 247 like they claim

They’re not tried to make customer to satisfy!!! I told them tried to get back the device sent it to wrong address. I can never get it back from other company. That’s why I called t-mobile to help to do something for me but they don’t want to do anything with it. Their service is SUCKS!!!

I switched from AT&T after being promised lower charges by T Mobile. At the time we were told that my wife also qualified for a "free" Samsung watch. I now get the bill and its higher than my AT&T bill was. When I queried this with T Mobile they said that the additional charges were due to the "free" watch. It's also too late to return the watch. Be careful of this company - they are RIPOFF artists. And their customer service sucks too!!!

Hello everyone, i feel very disappointed that T-Mobile start had a bad customer service.  Not like before when you call 611 you can get Customer on the phone quickly or Automictic said press number 1 call you back or number 2 stay on the line. But at this moment they change their policy and let you wait on the line average 45minutes to 3 hours wait time. I very disappointed and i will stop the T-Mobile services and my family. Will change different provider. This is today date May, 7 2024. Bad Services hope this messages will come to the damn Owner.

T-Mobile ripped me off.T-Mobile ripped me off. The sales tec for t-mobile said if we upgraded to go 5 we would get phones so we did. They are billing me full retail for the phones 2 months later. I have been through 5 support people and managers with no results. They are thieves. They are telling me that they do not need to stand behind there sales tech. I did a Google search and this has happened to lots of other people. This is there business plan.

Do not go to the T Mobile store on Spartanburg Hwy Hendersonville NC. An employee named Conner waited on us He acted like we interrupted whatever he was doing in back room. We wanted to upgrade our phones and we were told that their service was down and that the store was completely out of phones. That was our experience.

Switching to T-Mobile was the worst decision I have made in a long time. The single worst customer service I have ever received from any company period. Promised discounts not delivered. Call after call over months ended in repeated broken promises. It was clear that the rep either had no access to the history of previous conversations or did not care to look. Really seems like they have zero idea what they are doing. I’m on my way back to Verizon just as soon as I can get T-Mobile’s incompetent team to process unlocks on my phones. Don’t get lured in by the lower cost. This company is a nightmare to deal with, right down to their cringey fake “we got you” dialogue.
Worst promise, free phones end up they did not honor their contract so I am paying for the phones paying for the activation. They are a bunch of scumbags.

I responded to a promo. It took 3 days to get results. Long waits on calls and hangup. When I did get to speak to a rep they would spend most of their time asking me to wait. The web site was even worse and basically does not work. When all was said and done the $40 promo cost me $60.

I had Verizon for a year and half and I never had any data problems. My phone or hotspot is always slow. I switched to -mobile because they’d pay off my phone as a promotion. It’s $50 cheaper than Verizon but everything is so slow. I regret switching providers. You get what you pay for I guess. Imma get a Ryan Reynolds’s phone!
Was overcharged and support refuses to refund what they overcharged me, including their hidden fees. Also coverage and signal is garbage. They should remove 5G/6G and go back to to LTE/4G.

Worse customer service I've ever experienced. When you need customer service they push you to self help like all of them. Submitted an unlock request phone completely paid off. Spoke with two different reps and both said it would take a week and they would call me back neither did. Call back in spoke with supervisor named Mary. She apologize and said it would be unlocked in 48 hrs. After 2 weeks I called back and spoke with  a Harrison. He stated he needed to reverify everything and wanted to know why I wanted my phone unlocked. I told him I already spoke with supervisor and was approved for unlock. He refused. I requested a call back f4om Mary or cancel the service. He immediately canceled my service. Horrible customer service. Never a warm transfer. Never read or I guess put notes on the account. Every rep will tell you something different. After signing up you're on your own.  Buyer beware!!!!!!

My family and I have been T-Mobile customers for over 20 years. On April 15 2024 my iPhone 13 Pro suddenly went into SOS mode. Meaning my phone lost service. I have not been connected to the T-Mobile network that I pay for since then. Since April 15, I have spent countless hours everyday, basically spending every moment of free time that I have calling T-Mobile tech-support to resolve this issue. I have been on multiple calls with Verizon and T-Mobile to resolve this issue. Apparently my IMEI number was blocked by Verizon, although I have never been a Verizon customer, like I mentioned above, my family, and I have only ever been T-Mobile customers for decades. Verizon has confirmed that they have unblocked my IMEI, they even confirm that with a T-Mobile supervisor on a conference call with me. I was told by the tech associate that they would call me back on a working line, knowing that my phone didn’t work, and apparently they called my blocked phone line and left a message which is another example of the carelessness and neglect of T-Mobile and my issue. Every single time I call I’m having to re-explain myself and I’ve been extremely patient with each and every T-Mobile representative I’ve spoken to never raising my voice and always respectful, but I am at my absolute limit. After multiple T-Mobile representatives have promised me with callbacks with solutions and never hearing back. I feel like this company doesn’t care about this major inconvenience, stress causing, and dangerous situation this issue has become. As a young woman in my 20s, not having any cell phone service for over a week while having to work an hour away from home has made me feel extremely unsafe and uncared for by T-Mobile as a company. I am traveling in two days and feel immensely anxious and unsafe with the idea of being out of state with no phone service. If something happens to me during my travels, how will I be able to contact my family? How is that okay?  This has affected my every day peace of mind. At this point, my entire family is ready to leave T-Mobile due to the absolute disappointment of the handling of this issue. I don’t believe it’s fair that I would have to pay for an upgrade for a new phone, which is what I’ve been told is my only immediate solution, when I have a iPhone 13 Pro in perfect condition. I am being forced to pay a new phone due to T-Mobile’s inability to solve an issue that is not my fault? That is unfair. I was told I was eligible for a warranty trade-in and then within 24 hours told I was not due to the issue being escalated and having to be seen through.I don’t think a $20 credit or a warranty replacement is worth all the time and energy I have put in by trying to fix this issue, especially as an extremely loyal T-Mobile customer, who has done nothing but treat the T-Mobile staff with upmost respect during this very frustrating issue, after the countless promises of call backs and solutions that not a single T-Mobile associate has kept.

T-Mobile says iPhone on us. That means free to the consumer. They deceive you with statement credits which require you to stay with them for 24 months or you pay full price for the phone, now your a hostage. It took 3 weeks to receive a "free phone" just to find out it's not truly free. If it was free, I'd be free to leave anytime due to poor CS , poor signals and outright crappy overall verbage from their " global partners" poor understanding of the English language. There's no freedom in Amerika or with T-Mobile. If your looking for more deceptive practices in your life, hook up with Satan and his minions at T-Mobile. You'll get with anger.
Terrible company.  Stole $55 from me for internet service I never used

edits.text: T Mobile lied about my the home internet working at my address.  Completely wasted my time
Salesmen said I’d have 300Mbps speed.  I only had 22.  Complete waste of my time and don’t appreciate being lied to

text:
T-Mobile has deceptive trade practices, fraudulent ads and guarantees which they do not honor, and their websites and customer service agents lie.
I thought you folks would be interested in the recent experience I had with T-Mobile.
    Having spotty cell service with our current cell carrier, I was looking at competitive services on my smartphone on April 6 and I came across T-Mobile. Their pricing is quite competitive. I then came across a feature on their phone accessed web site that pushed me to try them.
    The Go Back Guarantee: On the phone version of their website, they had a large ad, “You'll love it here, guaranteed. With our new $50 Go Back Guarantee, if you're not satisfied within 30 days, you can switch back to your old carrier and get $50 for every voice line. Via virtual prepaid Master card. Allow 5 weeks for card. Get full terms (hyperlink)”
    The Go Back Guarantee Full Terms: “You'll love it here, guaranteed. Contact us before cancelling or porting out line(s) to request payout. Limited time offer; subject to change. Port-in voice line from postpaid plan w/ eligible carrier (i.e., AT&T, Verizon, Spectrum, Claro, Liberty, Xfinity or US Cellular), and port-out to eligible carrier within 30 days of T-Mobile line activation required. Account must be in good standing (e.g., final bill paid) to receive virtual prepaid MasterCard (no cash access & expires in 6 months). Up to 12/account. One offer per subscriber. Not available for customers that cancelled T-Mobile service within the past 90 days. Card is issued by Sunrise Banks N.A., Member FDIC, pursuant to a license from Mastercard(sic) International Incorporated. Use of this card constitutes acceptance of the terms and conditions stated in the Cardholder Agreement.”
    I showed my wife and her sister this guarantee and they agreed it was worth a try.
    I went to their store on April 7 and got their service.
    By April 11, it was evident T-Mobile wasn't working for us.
    I went to the store who told me to contact Customer Care by phone.
    The Customer Care agent eventually handed me over to a supervisor who repeated what the CC agent was telling me, “This guarantee promotion didn't start until today (April 11) and cannot be honored for service purchased prior.” The supervisor claimed he didn't see the Go Back Guarantee on the website even though I told him where it was within the web site to see it, but he claimed he didn't see it.  He said T-Mobile could not honor the guarantee.  Per the guarantee, I had yet to cancel T-Mobile, nor had I started service with another carrier.
    Since then, the guarantee has been removed from the desktop website, though it still exists on the phone website.  Regardless, T-Mobile refuses to back up the advertised guarantee.
    Per T-Mobile's advertised guarantee, they owe me $100 (for two lines), a guarantee they refuse to honor.
    How many others have had the 'promotion' not start until AFTER their service has started???

Was Paid ahead when I switched to Mint mobile. I still had at least 15 days left when I cancelled T-Mobile. I just figured that money was my loss. Cancelled the middle of March Then I received 2 bills on the same day, one dated April 5th for $41.44 and one dated April 10th for $13.82. The second one had a $7.00 late fee as part of it. Called them and the guy was supposed to call me back within 2 days. Never happened. I just called them back. I got nowhere. anyway, I paid the $13.82 and told her I need to get off of the phone and go leave a bad review. They don't like that. Then she was going to give me the $7.00 late fee back but she found out she cant do that on an account that was cancelled. Anyway do what you will but I don't recommend them.
